title: Smoky Quesadilla-Style Andalusian Deodeok-Root Tarta de Santiago
description: A smoky, glassy-jelly Andalusian dessert featuring deodeok-root, perfect for a kid-friendly Spanish meal, grilled to perfection in a robata-grill.
instructions: In a blender, puree the Deodeok root until smooth.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the almond meal, pistachio meal, coconut sugar, and a pinch of salt (if using).
Add the melted coconut oil, egg whites, lemon zest, smoked paprika (if using), and pureed Deodeok root to the bowl, and mix until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch (6 mm).
Brush the robata-grill with a small amount of melted coconut oil, and place the dough on the grill.
Cook for 4 minutes, then flip the tarta and cook for an additional 4 minutes, until golden brown.
Transfer the tarta to a wire rack to cool slightly, then cut into wedges and serve.

To make the filling, combine the water, Deodeok root puree, and a pinch of salt in a small saucepan, and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and forms a glassy-jelly consistency, about 10-12 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
To assemble the tarta, place a spoonful of the Deodeok root filling onto one half of the tarta, then fold the other half over to enclose the filling.
Brush the edges with a small amount of melted coconut oil, and press gently to seal the tarta.
Cut into wedges and serve immediately.
